title: excoecaria acerifolia var. genuina
intro: Excoecaria acerifolia var. genuina, also known as the False Maple or Poison Tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its maple-like leaves and toxic sap. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties. The health benefits of Excoecaria acerifolia var. genuina include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant is often prepared as a herbal remedy by extracting its sap or leaves, which can be used in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
